title: Responsive Design with Frameworks Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the responsive design features of modern CSS frameworks.

questions.question: What is the core principle of modern CSS framework design for responsiveness?
In a 12-column grid system, which class would make an element take half the screen width on medium screens?
Which of these is NOT a common breakpoint provided by CSS frameworks?
Tailwind CSS uses a prefix to enable responsive utility classes. What is the syntax for a medium screen width?
What's the main advantage of using a CSS framework's grid system instead of raw CSS Grid?
options: It provides pre-defined, tested breakpoints and utility classes.
It's the only way to make websites responsive.
It's more lightweight than standard CSS.
